explanation: Contradictory statements detected from the same speaker across turns
conversation_text: Hey B, I was reading this thing online about how kids these days are getting vaccines for everything under the sun. My grandma always said that stuff messes with your natural defenses. What do you think? Should we even bother? Uh, yeah, I mean... my family never did much of that when I was growing up. Grandpa swore by the old ways – honey and garlic for colds, you know? Tradition kept us alive for generations. Exactly! Like, why fix what ain't broke? Ancestors didn't have all these shots and we're still here. Remember that story your dad told about great-grandpa surviving the flu without doctors? Oh yeah, totally. He just used elderberry tea and prayed a bit. Passed down from his pa. Science folks say it's risky, but... our bloodline's proof it works, right? No need for their fancy trials. Haha, pray a bit – that's the wisdom right there. My auntie still does the same for her kids. Says modern medicine's just poisoning us slow. What about those autism links people whisper about? Grandma warned us.
